skill.name: writing-fragments
skill.description: Runs an interview-style writing session that captures claims, scenes, quotes, and half-formed ideas as reusable article fragments.
skill.summary: Capture raw writing fragments in a single Markdown document before outlining a draft.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution, weak-cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from Markdown text and examples. The skill has low risk because it intentionally reads and appends to a user-selected Markdown file.

low_findings.title: Intended Markdown File Read And Append
low_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to re-read and append to a user-selected document. This is expected behavior, but it gives the skill filesystem access during use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and append behavior is explicit in the skill instructions. It is limited to a user-provided path and does not show destructive intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Command Execution Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby shell backtick locations are a Markdown code fence and a literal delimiter example. No executable shell command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation for the output file format. They do not instruct Ruby execution or shell command execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The cited description line contains writing guidance only. No hash function, encryption call, or cryptographic workflow appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter prose about writing fragments. I found no cryptographic API or weak algorithm usage in the cited text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The cited line asks the user where to save a document if no path was provided. It does not collect system inventory or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line requests a destination path for the output file. There is no evidence of reconnaissance, enumeration, or data exfiltration.

content.user_title: Capture Writing Fragments for Later Drafts
content.value_statement: Early ideas often disappear before they become useful writing. This skill interviews you and stores strong fragments in one Markdown file.
actual_capabilities: Runs an open-ended interview to surface article ideas.
Captures claims, scenes, quotes, complaints, and half-thoughts.
Appends new fragments to one Markdown document.
Re-reads the document before writing to preserve user edits.
Keeps fragments separated by horizontal rules without imposing an outline.
Can revise, cut, or merge specific fragments when asked.
limitations: It does not create a complete article outline by default.
It needs a file path before it can save fragments.
It depends on the assistant having permission to read and write the target file.
It does not fact-check claims captured during the session.

use_cases.title: Prepare Essay Raw Material
use_cases.description: Collect early claims, examples, and memorable lines before deciding the final structure.

use_cases.title: Capture Product Narrative Ideas
use_cases.description: Interview a founder or product lead and preserve useful language for future positioning work.

use_cases.title: Develop Technical Article Fragments
use_cases.description: Record observations, code scenarios, and explanations that may later become a technical post.

prompt_templates.title: Start A Fragment Session
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin with a broad topic and a destination file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me collect writing fragments about [topic]. Save them to [path]. Interview me and append useful material as we talk.

prompt_templates.title: Mine A Rough Idea
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn a vague idea into several usable pieces.
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a rough idea about [topic]. Ask focused questions and capture claims, examples, and sharp sentences as fragments.

prompt_templates.title: Develop A Specific Argument
prompt_templates.scenario: Explore one thesis without creating an outline.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me develop fragments around this claim: [claim]. Do not outline yet. Capture strong language and useful objections.

prompt_templates.title: Refine An Existing Fragment File
prompt_templates.scenario: Continue working with a file that already contains fragments.
prompt_templates.prompt: Open [path], preserve my edits, and continue the fragment session. Cut, merge, or sharpen fragments when I ask.

output_examples.input: I want to write about why teams confuse speed with progress.
output_examples.output: A Markdown document starts with a working title and adds short fragments about rushed work, misleading metrics, and delayed learning.

output_examples.input: That last idea is too soft. Make it sharper.
output_examples.output: The previous fragment is revised into a more direct sentence while the rest of the document is preserved.

output_examples.input: Add the story about the launch meeting, but keep it rough.
output_examples.output: A new fragment is appended with the launch meeting scene and a short note about why it matters.

best_practices: Give a clear file path at the start of the session.
Say when a thought should be captured, cut, merged, or sharpened.
Review the fragment file during the conversation and edit it directly when useful.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when you need a finished article in one pass.
Do not treat captured fragments as verified facts.
Do not point it at a file that contains unrelated important content.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or updates a Markdown file containing raw writing fragments for later drafting.

faq.question: Does it make an outline?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed to collect raw material before structure is imposed.

faq.question: Can I edit the file during the session?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill tells the assistant to re-read the file before each write.

faq.question: What kinds of fragments can it capture?
faq.answer: It can capture claims, examples, scenes, quotes, lists, complaints, and unfinished thoughts.

faq.question: Does it overwrite my document?
faq.answer: It is instructed to preserve edits and append new fragments unless you ask for a specific edit.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
